name: Good Morning
instructions: Place a barbell on your upper traps (like a back squat). Stand with feet hip‑width apart, knees slightly bent. Keeping your back neutral and chest up, hinge at the hips and push your butt back, allowing your torso to descend until it is nearly parallel to the floor (or until you feel a strong hamstring stretch). Do not let your lower back round. Return to the upright position by driving your hips forward.
form_cues: Keep your back flat at all times
Only go as low as you can maintain a neutral spine
Knees stay slightly bent, not straight
Push your hips back, not just bend over
safety_notes: Use a very light weight or just the bar to start. This exercise puts significant stress on the lower back. If you feel any sharp pain, stop immediately. It is not suitable for those with existing lumbar spine issues.
common_mistakes: Not pushing hips back far enough
